title: Statement on the comments from Secretary of War Pete Hegseth
why_it_matters: Follow-up: Dario Amodei's direct official statement responding to Secretary Hegseth escalates the Anthropic–Pentagon standoff to CEO-level public confrontation. This is a new development in a story with major implications for AI companies' relationship with the US military.

title: Granite 4.0 1B Speech: Compact, Multilingual, and Built for the Edge
why_it_matters: IBM drops a 1B-parameter multilingual speech model designed for edge deployment. Compact enough to run on-device, which matters as speech AI moves off the cloud and onto phones and embedded hardware.

title: LeRobot v0.5.0: Scaling Every Dimension
why_it_matters: HuggingFace's open-source robotics framework hits v0.5.0 with major scaling improvements. LeRobot is becoming the default toolkit for AI robotics researchers who want a PyTorch-native pipeline.

title: Ulysses Sequence Parallelism: Training with Million-Token Contexts
why_it_matters: HuggingFace details Ulysses SP, a technique for training models with million-token context windows by parallelizing across sequence dimensions. Practical infrastructure knowledge for anyone training long-context models.

title: How to run Qwen 3.5 locally
why_it_matters: Follow-up: Unsloth publishes a practical guide to running the Qwen 3.5 family locally with quantization and fine-tuning tips. Useful companion piece now that the initial hype has settled and people want to actually deploy it.

title: Iran's attacks on Amazon data centers in UAE, Bahrain signal a new kind of war
why_it_matters: Physical attacks on cloud infrastructure in the Middle East signal that AI compute is now a strategic military target. If you run workloads in those regions, this is a wake-up call for geo-redundancy planning.

title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater
why_it_matters: MIT Tech Review examines how AI-powered OSINT dashboards and real-time intelligence tools are turning geopolitical conflict into a spectator sport, raising questions about the line between information and entertainment.

title: Production query plans without production data
why_it_matters: Simon Willison shares a practical technique for generating realistic database query plans without needing production data. Directly useful for developers optimizing SQL in staging environments.
action: Try the technique on your staging DB

title: The Download: murky AI surveillance laws, and the White House cracks down on defiant labs
why_it_matters: Follow-up: MIT Tech Review covers the legal gray zone around Pentagon AI surveillance of Americans and the White House escalating pressure on AI labs that resist government partnerships. Adds legal analysis to the Anthropic/DoD story.

title: Perhaps not Boring Technology after all
why_it_matters: Simon Willison revisits the 'choose boring technology' philosophy in the age of AI tooling, arguing the calculus has changed when AI can flatten learning curves. A useful framing for teams debating tech stack choices.
